Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user has provided a current model that is underfitting meaning it allows too many invalid sequences

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at the pseudocode it creates a sequence where after creating and approving the requisition there is a parallel block containing Create Purchase Order Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO and Pay Invoice Then it closes the case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the main issue here is that parallel execution allows these activities to happen in any order For example the model would permit paying the invoice before receiving the goods or the invoice itself

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me recall the standard Procure to Pay flow 1 Create requisition 2 Approve requisition 3 Create PO 4 Receive goods 5 Receive invoice 6 Match invoice to PO 7 Pay invoice 8 Close case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the critical dependencies are Approval must come before PO creation PO creation must come before goods receipt and invoice receipt Goods receipt and invoice receipt must both happen before matching Matching must happen before payment Payment must happen before closing

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The current parallel block is the root of all evil here It lets Receive Goods Receive Invoice etc all run independently without proper sequencing

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: I should structure this as a strict sequence with necessary parallelism only where allowed Specifically After requisition approval we must create the PO first sequence Then we can receive goods and receive invoice in parallel since both can happen independently after PO creation But matching requires both goods receipt and invoice receipt to be done Then payment must come after matching Finally close case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the corrected structure should be 1 Sequence Create Req Approve Req 2 Then Create PO 3 Then a parallel block for Receive Goods Receive Invoice 4 Then Match Invoice 5 Then Pay Invoice 6 Then Close Case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: matching cant happen until both goods and invoice are received So after the parallel block of receiving goods and invoice we need a sequence for matching

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But wait is Create PO really a separate step that must be sequential Yes because you cant receive goods against a PO that doesnt exist So PO creation must come after requisition approval and before any receiving

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The parallel block for receiving goods and invoice is correct because they are independent events that both need to occur before matching

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the corrected tree structure would be SEQUENCE SEQUENCE Create Req Approve Req Create PO PARALLEL Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice Pay Invoice Close Case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me verify against the standard flow Create req Approve req Create PO Receive goods AND Receive invoice Match Pay Close Yes that matches the intended logic

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The parallel block ensures that receiving goods and invoice can happen in any order relative to each other but both must complete before matching Then matching must happen before payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now why was the original model bad Because it put Create PO Receive Goods etc all in parallel allowing for example Receive Goods before Create PO Pay Invoice before Receive Goods Match Invoice to PO before Receive Goods or Receive Invoice

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The new model fixes this by Making PO creation sequential after approval Only allowing receiving goods and invoice to be parallel to each other not to PO creation or matching Forcing matching to happen after both receives Then payment and close in sequence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also note that the original model had the parallel block containing all activities from Create PO to Pay Invoice which was way too permissive

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Another thing to check should Create PO be in parallel with anything No because it must happen after approval and before receiving goods So it must be sequential Similarly Match Invoice must happen after both receives so it cant be parallel with receiving

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This enforces Strict sequence for requisition creation approval PO creation Parallel but independent receiving of goods and invoice Mandatory matching after both receives Strict sequence for payment and closing

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This prevents PO creation before requisition approval Receiving goods before PO creation Receiving invoice before PO creation Matching before both receives Payment before matching Closing before payment

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Yes that should do it
